skill.name: rn-styling
skill.description: Guidance for React Native styling with NativeWind utilities, BrandColors tokens, responsive layouts, safe areas, and platform-specific UI patterns.
skill.summary: Apply React Native styling patterns with NativeWind and centralized BrandColors tokens.
skill.author: CJHarmath

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns. Review found these are false positives from Markdown code fences, React Native wording, and file structure examples, with no executable scripts, network access, secret access, or prompt injection found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fence Backticks
low_findings.description: The external command alerts are false positives. The cited lines are TypeScript examples and Markdown fences for React Native styling guidance, not executable Ruby backticks or shell execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are documentation examples and Markdown delimiters. No shell command, subprocess API, or executable script is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts are false positives. The cited text mentions React Native and a designSystem file name, not MD5, SHA-1, DES, or cryptographic operations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain descriptive metadata and a recommended file structure entry. There is no cryptographic algorithm usage or security-sensitive computation.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Pattern
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance alerts are false positives. The cited lines describe a styling system and example component paths, not host enumeration or environment discovery.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The word system appears in design-system context only. No commands, APIs, or instructions gather machine, user, process, network, or filesystem inventory.

content.user_title: Style React Native Apps with NativeWind
content.value_statement: React Native styling often mixes themes, utilities, and platform rules. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code practical styling patterns.
actual_capabilities: Explains when to use BrandColors and when to use NativeWind classes.
Shows theme-aware color patterns for light and dark React Native screens.
Documents class ordering behavior for NativeWind and conditional class names.
Covers safe area, keyboard avoiding, responsive layout, and platform-specific styles.
Provides guidance for animated styles and StyleSheet versus inline style choices.
limitations: Does not include runnable scripts, generators, or automated style migration tools.
Assumes a React Native or Expo project with NativeWind available or planned.
BrandColors names must be adapted to each app's actual design tokens.
NativeWind version differences still require checking the project's installed version.

use_cases.title: Standardize App Colors
use_cases.description: Replace scattered color usage with BrandColors tokens while keeping layout utilities in NativeWind.

use_cases.title: Build Responsive Mobile Screens
use_cases.description: Apply safe area, keyboard, breakpoint, and platform patterns to screens that work across devices.

use_cases.title: Review Styling Consistency
use_cases.description: Check components for class ordering, hardcoded colors, shadow handling, and theme-aware styling gaps.

prompt_templates.title: Choose Styling Approach
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner guidance for a single component
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this React Native component and explain which styles should use BrandColors and which should use NativeWind.

prompt_templates.title: Add Theme Awareness
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate update for light and dark mode
prompt_templates.prompt: Update this screen plan to support light and dark themes using BrandColors and React Native theme hooks.

prompt_templates.title: Fix Platform Layout Issues
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced layout review for mobile devices
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this Expo screen for safe area, keyboard avoiding, responsive layout, and iOS or Android style differences.

prompt_templates.title: Create Styling Standards
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced team standards and review checklist
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a project styling checklist based on NativeWind utilities, BrandColors tokens, animation style arrays, and platform-specific styles.

output_examples.input: A card component uses hardcoded colors and mixed spacing styles.
output_examples.output: Recommendation: keep layout spacing in NativeWind, move semantic colors to BrandColors, and place override classes after defaults.

output_examples.input: A form screen overlaps the keyboard on iOS and has uneven Android spacing.
output_examples.output: Recommendation: use KeyboardAvoidingView, tune the iOS offset, keep the scroll area flexible, and apply platform-specific padding.

output_examples.input: A dashboard should support phones and tablets.
output_examples.output: Recommendation: combine NativeWind responsive classes with window width checks when layout structure changes by device size.

best_practices: Use BrandColors for semantic colors and NativeWind for layout, spacing, borders, and radius.
Place component override classes after default classes so NativeWind conflict resolution is predictable.
Use style arrays for animated values and reusable complex styles.
anti_patterns: Do not scatter hardcoded brand colors across React Native components.
Do not assume every web Tailwind utility works the same way in NativeWind.
Do not use object spreads for animated styles that change on each render.

faq.question: Does this skill run commands or modify files?
faq.answer: No. It is documentation guidance for React Native styling patterns.

faq.question: Which projects benefit most from this skill?
faq.answer: Expo and React Native projects using NativeWind, centralized color tokens, or shared UI conventions benefit most.

faq.question: Can it replace a design system?
faq.answer: No. It helps apply design-system ideas, but teams still need their own tokens and component standards.

faq.question: Does it support dark mode?
faq.answer: Yes. It describes a BrandColors and BrandColorsDark pattern with React Native color scheme detection.

faq.question: Does it cover platform differences?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for shadows, safe areas, keyboard handling, and iOS or Android style choices.

faq.question: Is NativeWind required?
faq.answer: The guidance is written for NativeWind workflows, but several React Native styling principles apply without it.
